Traffic Light Controller

cfg_sync — Entity

Source file: src/cfg_sync.vhd.

ENTITY cfg_sync IS
Generics

Generic

Type

Default

Description
Ports

Port

Type

Mode

Description

sys_clk

std_logic

in

System clock domain (50 MHz).

sys_rst

std_logic

in

Synchronous active-high reset (sys_clk domain).

cfg_clk

std_logic

in

Config clock domain (slow bus clock).

cfg_rst

std_logic

in

Synchronous active-high reset (cfg_clk domain).

mode_sel

std_logic

in

Config inputs (cfg_clk domain). Blinky enable: 1 = blinky LED active.

duty_cycle

std_logic_vector(7 downto 0)

in

PWM duty cycle: 0x00 = 0 %, 0xFF = 100 %.

blinky_en

std_logic

out

Outputs (sys_clk domain). Synchronised blinky enable (two-flop).

pwm_duty

std_logic_vector(7 downto 0)

out

PWM duty cycle (unsynchronised crossing).

Annotated Source

src/cfg_sync.vhd
  1-- Configuration synchroniser.
  2--
  3-- Receives runtime configuration from a slow config bus clock domain
  4-- (cfg_clk, e.g. 10 MHz from an external microcontroller) and makes it
  5-- available in the main system clock domain (sys_clk, 50 MHz).
  6--
  7-- mode_sel   crosses via a two-flop synchroniser (safe).
  8-- duty_cycle crosses as a raw multi-bit bus with no synchronisation
  9--            (unsafe — included to demonstrate CDC detection; a real
 10--            design should use a handshake or grey-code scheme here).
 11library ieee;
 12use ieee.std_logic_1164.all;
 13
 14entity cfg_sync is
 15    port (
 16        -- System clock domain (50 MHz).
 17        sys_clk    : in  std_logic;
 18        -- Synchronous active-high reset (sys_clk domain).
 19        sys_rst    : in  std_logic;
 20        -- Config clock domain (slow bus clock).
 21        cfg_clk    : in  std_logic;
 22        -- Synchronous active-high reset (cfg_clk domain).
 23        cfg_rst    : in  std_logic;
 24        -- Config inputs (cfg_clk domain).
 25        -- Blinky enable: 1 = blinky LED active.
 26        mode_sel   : in  std_logic;
 27        -- PWM duty cycle: 0x00 = 0 %, 0xFF = 100 %.
 28        duty_cycle : in  std_logic_vector(7 downto 0);
 29        -- Outputs (sys_clk domain).
 30        -- Synchronised blinky enable (two-flop).
 31        blinky_en  : out std_logic;
 32        -- PWM duty cycle (unsynchronised crossing).
 33        pwm_duty   : out std_logic_vector(7 downto 0)
 34    );
 35end entity cfg_sync;
 36
 37architecture rtl of cfg_sync is
 38
 39    -- cfg_clk domain registers
 40    signal mode_sel_r   : std_logic;
 41    signal duty_cycle_r : std_logic_vector(7 downto 0);
 42
 43    -- sys_clk domain: two-flop synchroniser pipeline for mode_sel_r
 44    signal mode_meta    : std_logic;
 45    signal mode_sync_r  : std_logic;
 46
 47begin
 48
 49    -- p_cfg_reg: Register config inputs in the cfg_clk domain.
 50    --
 51    -- Provides a stable registered version of mode_sel and duty_cycle
 52    -- before they cross into the sys_clk domain.
 53    p_cfg_reg : process(cfg_clk) is
 54    begin
 55        if rising_edge(cfg_clk) then
 56            if cfg_rst = '1' then
 57                mode_sel_r   <= '0';
 58                duty_cycle_r <= (others => '0');
 59            else
 60                mode_sel_r   <= mode_sel;
 61                duty_cycle_r <= duty_cycle;
 62            end if;
 63        end if;
 64    end process p_cfg_reg;
 65
 66    -- p_mode_sync: Two-flop synchroniser for mode_sel_r (cfg_clk → sys_clk).
 67    --
 68    -- mode_sel_r is sampled twice in the sys_clk domain to resolve
 69    -- metastability before the signal is used by downstream logic.
 70    --
 71    -- .. wavedrom::
 72    --
 73    --    { "signal": [
 74    --      { "name": "cfg_clk",    "wave": "p........" },
 75    --      { "name": "sys_clk",    "wave": "P........" },
 76    --      { "name": "mode_sel_r", "wave": "0...1...." },
 77    --      { "name": "mode_meta",  "wave": "0....1..." },
 78    --      { "name": "mode_sync_r","wave": "0.....1.." }
 79    --    ]}
 80    p_mode_sync : process(sys_clk) is
 81    begin
 82        if rising_edge(sys_clk) then
 83            if sys_rst = '1' then
 84                mode_meta   <= '0';
 85                mode_sync_r <= '0';
 86            else
 87                mode_meta   <= mode_sel_r;
 88                mode_sync_r <= mode_meta;
 89            end if;
 90        end if;
 91    end process p_mode_sync;
 92
 93    -- p_duty_capture: Capture duty_cycle_r directly in the sys_clk domain.
 94    --
 95    -- WARNING: This is an unsynchronised multi-bit crossing. duty_cycle_r is
 96    -- driven in the cfg_clk domain and sampled here without a handshake or
 97    -- grey-code scheme. Glitches are possible if both bits change together
 98    -- near a sys_clk edge. A proper implementation should use a handshake
 99    -- protocol or an async FIFO.
100    p_duty_capture : process(sys_clk) is
101    begin
102        if rising_edge(sys_clk) then
103            if sys_rst = '1' then
104                pwm_duty <= (others => '0');
105            else
106                pwm_duty <= duty_cycle_r;
107            end if;
108        end if;
109    end process p_duty_capture;
110
111    blinky_en <= mode_sync_r;
112
113end architecture rtl;
